enriched_points.expanded_content: Kafka Streams is a lightweight library for Kafka-centric stream processing, while Apache Flink is a comprehensive framework for advanced, stateful stream and batch processing.
Kafka Streams excels in simplicity and integration with Kafka; however, it may lack advanced features compared to Flink, which handles complex computations effectively but requires more setup.

Kafka Streams is ideal for lightweight applications needing rapid deployment within Kafka. Flink suits complex event processing, real-time analytics, and scenarios requiring extensive state management.
Kafka Streams integrates seamlessly within the Kafka ecosystem, while Flink offers extensive connectors for diverse data sources, allowing integration with various systems and storage solutions.
The future will see enhanced collaboration between Kafka and Flink, focusing on real-time analytics, advanced event processing, and improvements in scalability and fault tolerance.
